answer:
(i) Suppose that Marco has $n$ friends, and let $A_i$ be the set of stickers in the collection of friend $i$. Let $A = \bigcup_{i=1}^n A_i$. We will show that there exists a subset $S \subseteq \{1, 2, \ldots, n\}$ such that $|A_S| = n$, where $A_S = \bigcup_{i \in S} A_i$. 

We will use the principle of inclusion-exclusion. Let $|A_i| = a_i$ for each $i$. Then, by the principle of inclusion-exclusion, we have

$$|A| = \sum_{i=1}^n a_i - \sum_{i < j} a_i a_j + \sum_{i < j < k} a_i a_j a_k - \cdots + (-1)^{n+1} a_1 a_2 \cdots a_n.$$

Since $A \neq \bigcup_{i=1}^n A_i$, we have

$$|A| < \sum_{i=1}^n a_i - \sum_{i < j} a_i a_j + \sum_{i < j < k} a_i a_j a_k - \cdots + (-1)^{n+1} a_1 a_2 \cdots a_n.$$

Since $a_i \ge 0$ for each $i$, we have

$$\sum_{i=1}^n a_i - \sum_{i < j} a_i a_j + \sum_{i < j < k} a_i a_j a_k - \cdots + (-1)^{n+1} a_1 a_2 \cdots a_n \le \sum_{i=1}^n a_i.$$

Therefore, we have

$$|A| < \sum_{i=1}^n a_i.$$

Since $|A| \le \sum_{i=1}^n a_i$, we have

$$|A| = \sum_{i=1}^n a_i.$$

Since $|A| = \sum_{i=1}^n a_i$, there exists a subset $S \subseteq \{1, 2, \ldots, n\}$ such that $|A_S| = n$.

(ii) We will show that $n$ tables will always be sufficient for Marco to achieve his desire. Let $S_1, S_2, \ldots, S_n$ be the subsets of $\{1, 2, \ldots, n\}$ such that $|A_{S_i}| = n$ for each $i$. We will show that for each $i$, there exists a table $T_i$ such that $A_{S_i} \cap T_i = \emptyset$.

Suppose that $A_{S_i} \cap T_i \ne \emptyset$ for some $i$. Then, there exists $j \in S_i$ such that $A_j \cap T_i \ne \emptyset$. Since $A_j \cap T_i \ne \emptyset$, there exists $k \in T_i$ such that $k \in A_j$. Since $k \in A_j$, we have $A_j \subseteq A_{S_i}$. Therefore, $A_{S_i} \cap T_i \ne \emptyset$ implies that $A_{S_i} \subseteq T_i$. Since $|A_{S_i}| = n$, we have $T_i = A_{S_i}$. 

Since $T_i = A_{S_i}$, we have $A_{S_i} \cap T_j = \emptyset$ for all $j \ne i$. Therefore, we can assign $S_i$ to table $T_i$ for each $i$. This shows that $n$ tables will always be sufficient for Marco to achieve his desire.

\boxed{n}
